title: Quality Signal Model
baseline: Stage 5 closed the independent audit gate. Stage 6 should now make ABH see quality problems directly: product-quality signals first, agent navigation second. Existing drift, memory, route, and reporting plans need one shared signal model before runtime work begins.
goals: Define shared quality signal concepts for drift, reusable memory, verification freshness, audit outcomes, and project health.
Update the Stage 6 roadmap queue so quality-signal-model precedes drift-quality, memory-index, and project-health-report.
Document how drift findings, memory entries, route ranking, abh next, and health reports consume the shared quality signal fields.
Keep this slice planning/docs-only so later runtime changes can be implemented behind a stable model.
non_goals: Do not change runtime CLI, MCP, close, verification, audit, memory, route, or drift behavior in this slice.
Do not implement drift severity/confidence, memory indexing, route ranking, report commands, or abh next integration yet.
Do not introduce external services, LLM scoring, databases, release automation, or team policy.
exit_criteria: A Stage 6 quality signal model doc exists and defines severity, confidence, evidence references, excerpts, relationship fields, status, and health aggregation semantics.
development-roadmap and task-board describe Stage 6 as product-quality-first and agent-navigation-second.
Agent Protocol or codebase map records which future command surfaces will consume quality signals.
.abh/roadmap.json contains the four ordered Stage 6 queue items without preassigned plan ids.
